title: Is AI trading actually an edge or are we all just backtesting ourselves into false confidence?
author: Emergency-Buy-4339
selftext:
Backtesting looks great until it hits live markets. You optimize your model on historical data, hit 80% accuracy in testing, go live and watch it fall apart in the first week. The market already adapted, the pattern doesn't exist anymore, or your sample size was just too small to mean anything.

Genuine question for the people actually running automated systems — how much of what you built in backtesting actually translated to real performance? And for the AI-powered signal tools out there, how do you even verify the edge is real before risking actual money?

Curious where people actually stand on this because the gap between backtesting confidence and live trading reality seems like the thing nobody talks about honestly enough.

comments.author: richard-b-inya
comments.body: It's called curve fitting.  Humans are bad enough when they backtest, LLMs are going to super curve fit.

replies.author: Emergency-Buy-4339
replies.body:
Yeah exactly, with human touch it's bad enough,  you tweak parameters until the backtest looks beautiful and convince yourself you found something real. But you're right that LLMs could take this to a whole new level. They're incredibly good at finding patterns, which sounds great until you realize they'll find patterns in pure noise just as confidently as real signals.

The scary part is it'll look incredibly convincing. Clean backtest, logical reasoning, confident output. Underneath it's just an extremely sophisticated way of fooling yourself.

comments.author: RealisticBowl6353
comments.body: Built something out of this for fun, live tracks model with positive ev. Fun, but not a day job replacement.

replies.author: Emergency-Buy-4339
replies.body: It might be true actually what you say, but still keep in mind that its a whole world that develops all the time and i think at a certain moment it will become reality, everyday reality.

comments.author: jabberw0ckee
comments.body: I combine AI with Algo. The AI has access to all data and statistics and can guide traders which alerts have the highest probability.

comments.author: IndyJoeDv
comments.body: As soon as you say backtesting you're going to lose.  You MUST forward test. You can't test on data your model has trained on.  That's giving it the answers before the quiz.  Train on 3/4 of the data you have, then test on the 1/4 it's never seen before. Ideally it has multiple regime changes in both sets. Expect the results to be drastically different and if it's at 80% accuracy, something is still wrong, likely slippage or not accounting for fees. (or your data set is too small)
